zh.Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Ingham於大約1314年出生在英國諾福爾可英厄姆時，她的父親Sir Oliver Ingham29歲，母親Elizabeth Zouche40歲。她於1344年之前與Roger le Strange結婚。她去世於1377年在她的家鄉享年64歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可英厄姆。

zh-hans.Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England: 英国林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Ingham于大约1314年出生在英国諾福爾可英厄姆时，她的父亲Sir Oliver Ingham29岁，母亲Elizabeth Zouche40岁。她于1344年之前与Roger le Strange结婚。她去世于1377年在她的家乡享年64岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可英厄姆。

nameMeanings.name: Ingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from any of the places called Ingham, particularly in Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Suffolk. The placename is from the Old English personal name Inga + hām ‘homestead’. In Yorkshire this name was sometimes confused with Bingham in the 18th century.
English (of Norman origin): variant of the obsolete Norman surname Engaine; see Gain .

variants.name: Bingham
meanings: English (Dorset) and Irish (County Mayo): habitational name from Bingham (Nottinghamshire). The placename is probably from an Old English folk-name Bynningas (‘the people associated with a man named Bynna’), or possibly from an unattested Old English word bing ‘a kettle-shaped hollow’, + Old English hām ‘homestead’.
Irish (Ulster, of Scottish origin): altered form of   Bigham  .
American shortened and altered form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames such as   Bingenheimer  .

variants.name: Gain
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname for a cunning person, from Engaine, which is based on Old French engaigne ‘ingenuity, cunning, trickery’.

English: habitiational name from any of various places called after the Engain family, including Colne Engaine and Gaynes Park (Essex), d'Engaine's Farm (Cambridgeshire), and Aston Ingham (Hereford). Engaine (see 1 above) was the name of a powerful Norman family with lands in several counties. The original form of the surname has since died out, surviving as Gain and its variants. There has also been considerable confusion with Ingham .
French: nickname from a noun derivative of Old French gaainier ‘to obtain, gain, win’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Ingham was born about 1314, in Ingham, Norfolk, England, her father, Sir Oliver Ingham, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Zouche, was 40. She married Roger le Strange before 1344. She died in 1377, in her hometown, at the age of 64, and was buried in Ingham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Ingham est née environ 1314, à Ingham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Oliver Ingham, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Zouche, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé Roger le Strange avant 1344. Elle est décédée en 1377, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterrée à Ingham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Ingham um 1314 in Ingham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Oliver Ingham 29 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Zouche 40. Sie hat vor 1344 Roger le Strange geheiratet. Sie starb 1377 im Alter von 64 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Ingham, Norfolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Ingham è nata il circa 1314 nel Ingham, Norfolk, England, sua padre, Sir Oliver Ingham, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Zouche, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato Roger le Strange prima del 1344. Morì nel 1377, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolta a Ingham, Norfolk, England.

ja.Bedale, North Yorkshire, England: イギリスヨークシュアイアベダル
ja.lifeSummary: Joan Inghamが約1314年にイギリスノーフォルクインガムで生まれた時，父Sir Oliver Inghamは29歳，母Elizabeth Zoucheは40歳であった。1344年以前にRoger le Strangeと結婚した。彼女は1377年に故郷で64歳で死去し，イギリスノーフォルクインガムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Ingham(이)가 대략 1314년 영국 노르폴크 잉함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Oliver Ingham(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Zouche(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 1344년 이전 Roger le Strange(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1377년 그녀의 고향에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 잉함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Ingham nasceu aproximadamente 1314, em Ingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Oliver Ingham, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Zouche, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com Roger le Strange antes 1344. Ela faleceu em 1377, em sua cidade natal, com 64 anos, e foi sepultada em Ingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Ingham родилась около 1314 г. в местности ингхам, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Oliver Ingham было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Zouche, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Roger le Strange перед 1344 г. Она умерла в 1377 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 64 лет и была похоронена в местности ингхам, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Ingham nació aproximadamente 1314, en Ingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Oliver Ingham, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Zouche, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Roger le Strange antes de 1344. Murió en 1377, en su pueblo, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultada en Ingham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
